lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She took a deep breath before heading inside the prison.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Covered in barbed wires around the walls, it feels like something ominous lurks within...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She took a deep breath before heading inside the Prison.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Elsa visits her father in prison, watching as he slowly enters the visitor area.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ... I cannot even recognize him... he's... different...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She picked up the phone, her breath shaking as she spoke.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Dad...?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Dad? Is that you?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Dad, if that's you...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Why did you leave? Why did you abandon Mom?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Why did you abandon us? Why did you hurt us?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: I need answers.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: NOW!!!

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: Heh...

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: Haha...

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: ...Ha ha ha ha ha ha...

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: Oh you silly, stupid child. She wanted me to leave because your mother...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: W-what about my Mom...?

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: Your mother was a DISGUSTING, FILTHY HARLOT!

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: She kept you filthy RATS around while letting other men touched her!

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: You and your Sister are the fruit of disgrace!

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: I should’ve killed her from the moment you rats were born!

lines.speaker: Father
lines.text: HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA!!!!!!!!

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...May God forgive you...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Haunted by her Father's laughter, She hangs up and walks out of the prison in silence...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Now, let’s see what else do we have in here...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She noticed a covered and dusted item as she scrubbed it off, revealing an old photo book.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: A photo book? Looks... old and moldy.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Huh? What’s this?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She checked the Photo, where she noticed... herself?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Is this... Sister?

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She kept looking around the photo book, until she noticed a note on one of the pages:

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: “I’m sorry my children, but if God wills it...”

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...Mother...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Returning to her hometown, she heads to the church...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Seeking answers from the priest once again.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Father... I want to ask a question again...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Speak, my child. What troubles you today?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: ...Why does God let people hurt each other...?

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Oh... My child...

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Please, Father... I need to know

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: ... Very well. child.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: It’s because He is the loving, powerful and wise being.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: People suffer because it is the path that we chose.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: God provided us the freedom to choose. That includes choosing the rights and wrongs of a human being.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Every time we choose our actions, there is always a form of consequences...

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: But in the end, it's God who's gonna judge us in Judgement Day, my Child.

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: Does that mean... I choose wrong, Father?

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: That I was wrong to choose to be...

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Before she can speak again, the confessional begins to distort.

lines.speaker: Sister?
lines.text: Was it wrong to choose to be born??????????????

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: W-What...?

lines.speaker: Sister?
lines.text: ARE WE BORN WRONG BECAUSE OF OUR ORIGIN??????????

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: No... stop... please...

lines.speaker: Sister?
lines.text: IS THE SWEET FRUIT OF LIFE, TAINTED WITH PUTRIDNESS, DESERVING OF UNENDING HELL???????????????

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: STOP!! PLEASE!!! STOP TALKING!!!

lines.speaker: Elsa
lines.text: PLEASE!!!

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Save Her

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She prays and prays, words trembling from her lips.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The voice fades. The pain lingers. But she remains.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Until everything went Pitch. Black.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 2.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: She downs her pills with trembling hands.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The voice fades. The pain lingers. But she remains.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Until everything went Pitch. Black.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: End of Chapter 2.
